company.name: Arcutis Biotherapeutics
company.subtitle: ZORYVE $376M 2025 · Turned profitable · 2026 guide $480-495M · Topical derm franchise

market.notes: ZORYVE: $376M 2025 revenue, turned profitable. Approved for plaque psoriasis (cream), AD (cream 0.05%), scalp/body psoriasis (foam), seborrheic dermatitis (foam). Non-steroidal topical — addresses steroid phobia/fatigue. 2026 guide: $480-495M. Infant AD data positive (INTEGUMENT-INFANT). sNDA for infant AD planned.

us.reachPct.note: ZORYVE cream/foam (roflumilast) plaque psoriasis + seb derm + AD; launched Jul 2022 (psoriasis), 2023 (seb derm), 2024 (AD); $0.25B 2024 sales; ~30% reach of 8M US psoriasis/AD topical pool.

us.priceK.note: $0.9K/mo WAC ($725 for 60g); annual use ~$10K; Arcutis copay card $0 commercial, $50 Medicare cap.

us.wtpPct.note: ~65% -- commercial + Medicare Part D broad topical PDE4; PA required by most PBMs, copay card bridges; $0 copay commercial via Arcutis program.

eu.reachPct.note: ~15% EU5; EU launches 2024-25; Huadong Medicine ex-US partner for China; NICE TA pending.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~30% -- G-BA Zusatznutzen modest for topical PDE4 vs steroid generic; NICE TA gating.

row.reachPct.note: ~10% ROW; China Huadong partnership; Japan Sato Pharm; Canada launched 2023.

row.wtpPct.note: ~20% -- Canada PMPRB + Japan NHI topical derm; China NRDL 2026+.

us.patientsK.note: NPF: ~6M US psoriasis on topical-only therapy; mild-moderate 70% of PsO population.

eu.patientsK.note: EADV: ~10M EU27+UK topical-managed psoriasis; Dovobet/Enstilar vit-D + steroid combos standard.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~80M topical PsO; calcipotriol + betamethasone generics widespread.

us.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- commercial/Medicare cover Vtama (tapinarof) + Zoryve (roflumilast) + generic calcipotriol/steroids.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~40% -- NICE approved Vtama/Zoryve; generic vit-D analog + steroid combos dominant.

row.wtpPct.note: ~18% -- TCS generics + coal tar; novel topicals minimal; Japan maxacalcitol standard.

us.priceK.note: Blended $1.5K: Vtama ~$1.5K/tube + Zoryve $1K + Dovobet/Enstilar $400 + generic calcipotriol/steroid $50.

eu.priceK.note: ~$0.5K blended -- generic calcipotriol + steroid combos dominate; Vtama/Zoryve net ~$800.

penPct.note: ~10% peak -- non-steroidal novel topicals (Vtama, Zoryve) shift from chronic TCS use concerns.

assets.name: ZORYVE label expansions (infant AD + new formulations)

market.notes: INTEGUMENT-INFANT Phase 2 positive in 3-24 month infants. sNDA planned. Broadens addressable population across all age groups. Additional sensitive-area and formulation expansions possible.

us.patientsK.note: NEA: ~8M US AD on topicals; ~16% US adults had AD symptoms per NEA 2024; pediatric cohort large.

eu.patientsK.note: ETFAD: ~12M EU27+UK AD topical-managed; emollients + TCS + TCI stepwise.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~100M topical AD; emollients + TCS standard; Japan tacrolimus ointment widely used.

us.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- commercial/Medicaid cover Opzelura (ruxolitinib) + Eucrisa (crisaborole) + TCS generics; Zoryve emerging.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~45% -- NICE restrictive on Opzelura; generic TCS + TCI (Protopic/Elidel) mainstay.

row.wtpPct.note: ~20% -- TCS generics widely accessible; novel topicals minimal ex-Japan.

us.priceK.note: Blended $2K: Opzelura $2.5K/tube (~$15K/yr if chronic use) + Eucrisa $600/tube + TCS/TCI generics $50-200.

eu.priceK.note: ~$0.8K blended -- generic TCS/TCI dominant + selective Opzelura/Eucrisa net ~$8K.

penPct.note: ~12% peak -- novel non-steroidal topicals (Opzelura, Zoryve-AD, roflumilast) expanding long-term use.

us.reachPct.note: ZORYVE label expansions: scalp + body psoriasis (Zoryve topical foam 2023), pediatric AD (2024 sNDA), alopecia areata (pipeline); ~25% additional reach via label expansions.

us.priceK.note: $0.9K/mo WAC parity across expanded labels; volume discounts for high-script commercial.

us.wtpPct.note: ~60% -- label expansion leverages existing commercial + Medicare Part D; pediatric label gates Medicaid EPSDT broad coverage.

eu.reachPct.note: ~12% EU5 expansion indications; EMA label expansion post-US by 12-18mo.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~25% -- EU expansion post-HTA for each new indication.

market.notes: CD200R agonist fusion protein — novel mechanism for moderate-severe AD. First-in-human Phase 1a/1b enrolling. If it works: systemic AD therapy beyond dupilumab. Very early but diversifies beyond topicals.

us.patientsK.note: NEA: ~1.5M US moderate-severe AD on systemic therapy; ~16.5M US adults have AD symptoms total.

eu.patientsK.note: ETFAD: ~2.5M EU27+UK moderate-severe AD systemic-eligible; EASI/IGA severity scoring.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~20M mod-severe; Japan high prevalence + strong Dupixent uptake; China rapid growth.

us.wtpPct.note: ~65% -- Dupixent commercial/Medicare Part D; Rinvoq/Cibinqo JAKi; Adbry (tralokinumab); Ebglyss (lebrikizumab) new.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~48% -- NICE/G-BA cover Dupixent + JAKi restricted use (BBW); Adbry/Ebglyss rolling out.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- Japan J-DPS + China NRDL added Dupixent; cyclosporine generic + steroids baseline elsewhere.

us.priceK.note: Blended $38K: Dupixent $40K + Rinvoq $65K + Cibinqo $60K + Adbry $40K + Ebglyss $40K; cyclosporine generic $3K.

eu.priceK.note: ~$22K blended -- Dupixent net ~$25K + JAKi ~$30K; cyclosporine/MTX generic backbone.

row.priceK.note: ~$8K -- cyclosporine generic + phototherapy; biologics <15% penetration ex-Japan.

penPct.note: ~35% peak -- rapid biologic/JAKi expansion; OX40 (rocatinlimab, amlitelimab) pipeline adding options.

us.reachPct.note: ARQ-234 (nemolizumab-like IL-31 or OX40 biologic) atopic dermatitis systemic; Ph2 2025-26; ~2M US mod-severe AD post-Dupixent; launch 2029 ~10% reach.

us.priceK.note: ~$40K/yr projected; anchor to Dupixent $38K + Ebglyss $40K + Nemluvio.

us.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- AD biologic post-Dupixent pool; commercial + Medicare Part D; PA required post-Dupixent fail.

eu.reachPct.note: ~15% EU5 AD biologic; NICE TA Dupixent SOC; post-Dupixent biologic niche.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~22% -- EU AD biologic post-Dupixent access via G-BA.

row.reachPct.note: ~7% ROW; Japan NHI AD biologic broad; China NRDL Dupixent 2023.

market.notes: Topical JAK1 for alopecia areata. Avoids systemic JAK risks. Competitors: Olumiant, Litfulo (oral). If topical JAK works: safety advantage over oral. Phase 2 ongoing.

us.patientsK.note: National Alopecia Areata Foundation: ~300K US AA active; ~700K lifetime risk; ~20% severe (AT/AU).

eu.patientsK.note: EADV: ~450K EU27+UK AA active; SALT severity scale; autoimmune comorbidity common.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~4M AA; Japan JAK inhibitor early adopter; China limited novel access.

us.wtpPct.note: ~45% -- Olumiant (baricitinib, 2022) + Litfulo (ritlecitinib, 2023) Medicare/commercial; intralesional steroids off-label.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~32% -- NICE/G-BA approved Olumiant/Litfulo severe AA; topical/intralesional steroids standard baseline.

row.wtpPct.note: ~10% -- Japan approved baricitinib/ritlecitinib; topical minoxidil + steroids elsewhere.

us.priceK.note: Blended $42K: Olumiant $30K + Litfulo $50K (severe AA SALT>=50); topical minoxidil + steroids $500 baseline.

eu.priceK.note: ~$22K blended -- Olumiant net ~$18K + Litfulo ~$30K; restricted to severe.

row.priceK.note: ~$4K -- minoxidil + steroids + limited JAKi access in Japan.

penPct.note: ~20% peak -- JAKi class expanding (Rinvoq, deuruxolitinib pipeline); severe AA first, moderate expansion next.

us.reachPct.note: ARQ-255 topical deuruxolitinib alopecia areata; Ph1 2025; ~700K US AA; oral Olumiant/Litfulo/Leqselvi SOC; topical differentiator; launch 2030 ~15% reach.

us.priceK.note: ~$0.85K/mo topical projected; vs Olumiant $30K/yr oral, Litfulo $49K; topical undercut.

us.wtpPct.note: ~45% -- AA Rx commercial + Medicare Part D; topical AA novel, convenience premium vs oral JAK.

eu.reachPct.note: ~10% EU5 AA; NICE TA Olumiant 2022; topical AA novel EU.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~20% -- G-BA AA topical access limited; oral JAK entrenched.

row.reachPct.note: ~6% ROW; Japan NHI AA; China NRDL AA biologic limited.

market.notes: Topical JAK1 cream for hand eczema and vitiligo. Addresses unmet needs in difficult-to-treat areas. Phase 2/3 level data.

us.patientsK.note: American Vitiligo Research Foundation: ~1.5M US vitiligo; ~1% prevalence; non-segmental most common.

eu.patientsK.note: EADV VETF: ~2M EU27+UK vitiligo; VES/VASI severity; facial/hand involvement drives treatment demand.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~60M vitiligo; India highest prevalence + disease burden; stigma drives treatment demand.

us.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- Opzelura (ruxolitinib, 2022 first FDA vitiligo approval) commercial/Medicare; NB-UVB phototherapy covered.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~35% -- NICE approved Opzelura nonsegmental; phototherapy + topical steroids/TCI standard baseline.

row.wtpPct.note: ~12% -- topical steroids + NB-UVB; Opzelura access limited; India extensive phototherapy network.

us.priceK.note: Blended $8K: Opzelura $2.5K/tube chronic use ~$15K/yr (30% pts) + NB-UVB $3K/yr + TCS/TCI generic $200.

eu.priceK.note: ~$3K blended -- Opzelura net ~$10K + generic TCS/TCI + phototherapy.

row.priceK.note: ~$0.5K -- TCS/TCI generics + phototherapy; novel topicals minimal.

penPct.note: ~15% peak -- Opzelura first-in-class; upadacitinib + povorcitinib oral JAKi pipeline expanding severe cohort.

us.reachPct.note: ARQ-252 topical JAK hand eczema + vitiligo; Ph2 2024-25; ~10M US hand eczema + 2M vitiligo; Opzelura (ruxo) vitiligo SOC; launch 2029 ~10% reach.

us.priceK.note: ~$0.75K/mo topical; anchor to Opzelura $1.5K/mo WAC; ARQ-252 undercut for access.

us.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- Opzelura vitiligo precedent $18K/yr; topical JAK commercial + Medicare Part D; PA required.

eu.reachPct.note: ~12% EU5; NICE TA Opzelura pending; EMA topical JAK growing.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~22% -- G-BA Opzelura Zusatznutzen modest; EU topical JAK class access.

mega_bear.narrative: ZORYVE growth stalls + pipeline disappoints + derm market saturates
events: ✗ ZORYVE revenue plateaus at ~$450M — market ceiling for non-steroidal topical
✗ ARQ-234 Phase 1 safety/efficacy issue — systemic pipeline dead
✗ Topical JAK programs don't differentiate from oral JAKi
→ Single-franchise $450M revenue company, overvalued at $3B

bear.narrative: ZORYVE grows modestly + label expansions approved but limited upside
events: ✓ ZORYVE reaches $500-550M but growth slows to single digits
✓ Infant AD approved but incremental revenue
✗ ARQ-234 early data mixed — needs redesign
→ Profitable derm company at 6-7x revenue

base.narrative: ZORYVE franchise $700M+ + ARQ-234 promising + topical JAK portfolio builds
events: ✓ ZORYVE continues 25-30% growth — $700M+ by 2028
✓ Infant AD sNDA approved — full lifecycle coverage
✓ ARQ-234 Phase 1 encouraging — pipeline beyond topicals
~ ARQ-255/252 advancing through Phase 2/3

bull.narrative: Non-steroidal derm franchise $1B+ + ARQ-234 systemic platform + multi-product company
events: ✓ ZORYVE $1B+ peak — dominant non-steroidal topical across derm
✓ ARQ-234 validates CD200R in AD — first-in-class systemic
✓ Topical JAK approved for AA + hand eczema + vitiligo
✓ 5+ approved products, $1.5B+ peak revenue

psychedelic_bull.narrative: Topical + systemic derm platform — Abbvie/Sanofi acquires at $60+
events: ✓ ZORYVE franchise $1.5B+ across all approved indications globally
✓ ARQ-234 first-in-class systemic — expands into moderate-severe AD
✓ Full derm portfolio: topical + oral + biologic — rare complete platform
✓ AbbVie or Sanofi acquires for $6-10B to own derm franchise

lastData.title: $376M 2025 revenue, profitable, 2026 guide $480-495M
points: AAD 2026: multiple ZORYVE presentations across indications
sNDA planned for infant AD based on INTEGUMENT-INFANT data
Non-steroidal positioning avoids steroid phobia — key adoption driver

nextCatalyst.title: 2026 revenue trajectory + infant AD sNDA + ARQ-234 Phase 1 data
thresholds: ARQ-234 Phase 1 safety/PK — first look at CD200R mechanism

risks: $3B market cap at 8x 2025 revenue — premium for a topical derm company
ZORYVE is non-steroidal topical — limited in moderate-severe disease
Topical market has ceiling — systemic biologics (dupilumab) dominate moderate-severe
ARQ-234 CD200R is very early — years from contribution
Topical JAK programs face competition from oral JAKi (Rinvoq, Cibinqo)
Dermatology commercial execution is competitive — many large players
Pipeline diversity is positive but each program is early-stage
Stock down from highs — market may be pricing in growth deceleration

catalysts.title: ARQ-234 CD200R agonist Phase 1 AD data
ZORYVE pediatric psoriasis PDUFA decision
catalysts._rationale: sNDA for ZORYVE cream 0.3% in pediatric psoriasis has PDUFA date of June 29, 2026

catalysts.title: ARQ-252 vitiligo and hidradenitis suppurativa program advancement decisions
catalysts._rationale: Company expects program advancement decisions in Q4 2026 for vitiligo and Q1 2027 for HS
